# Relay stage 3: Diagnose
Task: followup-ui-polish-alignment-and-spacing
Working directory: /tmp/nix-shell.0TxEfP/visual-relay/wt/b355188660a3/plan-20260624121456/followup-ui-polish-alignment-and-spacing

## Task input
# UI polish: ACTIVITY title alignment, attachment row spacing, minor cleanups

Small cosmetic follow-ups surfaced while reviewing the recent UI changes
(`fix-right-panel-collapsed-state` `54cff3d`, `remove-button-sometimes-cut-off` `f7ef26e`,
`make-resizer-look-nicer` `7808033`). None are functional bugs; each is a low-risk polish item.

## 1. ACTIVITY panel title is not vertically centered
In `src/VisualRelay.App/Views/Controls/ActivityColumn.axaml`, the header was reordered so the
`panelTitle` `TextBlock` ("ACTIVITY") now sits in the same `Auto`-height row as the taller
"Reveal" button (and the toggle button), but — unlike the Queue panel's title, which sets
`VerticalAlignment="Center"` — the ACTIVITY title has no `VerticalAlignment`, so it can render
top-aligned relative to the button. **Fix:** add `VerticalAlignment="Center"` to the ACTIVITY
`panelTitle` `TextBlock` so it matches the Queue panel and aligns with the Reveal button.

## 2. Attachment path text butts against the "Reveal" button
In `src/VisualRelay.App/Views/Controls/TaskDetailPanel.axaml`, the attachment item template was
changed from a Grid (which had `ColumnSpacing="6"`) to a `DockPanel`. The `Reveal` button now has
`Margin="0,0,6,0"` (gap only on its right), so the trimmed file-path `TextBlock` (the fill child)
sits directly against the left edge of `Reveal` with no gap. **Fix:** give the `Reveal` button
`Margin="6,0,6,0"` to restore the ~6px gap between the path text and the button. (The ellipsis
already prevents overlap; this is spacing only.)

## 3. Minor cleanups (optional, low priority)
- In `ActivityColumn.axaml` (the splitter from `make-resizer-look-nicer`), the three grip dots
  (`GripDot1/2/3`) each have an identical `:pointerover` setter. Collapse them into a single
  shared `Classes`-based selector to DRY the styles (keep the visual result identical). Only do
  this if it doesn't fight the existing tests that count named borders — otherwise leave as-is.
- In `tests/VisualRelay.Tests/TaskDetailRemoveButtonLayoutTests.cs`, the assertion failure
  messages still say "Grid"/cite line numbers from before the DockPanel change; update the
  wording to reference the DockPanel so a future failure isn't misleading. (Message text only —
  do not weaken the assertions.)

## How to verify
- The "ACTIVITY" header title is vertically centered, level with the Reveal button.
- In the attachments list, there is a visible gap between a (long, ellipsized) file path and the
  Reveal button.
- Existing layout/affordance tests stay green; visual result of the splitter is unchanged.

## Constraints
- VR is general-purpose; no project-specific assumptions. Files ≤300 lines; `./visual-relay check`
  green; Conventional Commit subjects. This is cosmetic only — do not change collapse/resize
  behavior, the DockPanel structure, or the splitter's appearance/hit area.


## Manifest
(not set yet)

Options differ on scope (skip DRY → lower risk, include DRY → cleaner code, alternate margin placement).",

result.answer:
I've now reviewed all the relevant files. Here is the evidence organized by each issue:

---

## Evidence for each issue

### 1. ACTIVITY panel title missing `VerticalAlignment="Center"`

**File:** `src/VisualRelay.App/Views/Controls/ActivityColumn.axaml`, lines 41–43

```xml
<TextBlock Grid.Column="2"
           Text="ACTIVITY"
           Classes="panelTitle"/>
```

No `VerticalAlignment` attribute. Compare with the **Queue panel** equivalent (`src/VisualRelay.App/Views/Controls/QueuePanel.axaml`, lines 15–18):

```xml
<TextBlock Grid.Column="0"
           Text="{Binding TaskListTitle}"
           Classes="panelTitle"
           VerticalAlignment="Center"/>
```

The `Grid.Row="0"` header row in ActivityColumn is `RowDefinitions="Auto"` (line 15), so it sizes to its tallest child — the `StackPanel` containing the Reveal button (with its `MinHeight="0"`, `Padding="10,3"`). The ACTIVITY `TextBlock` has no vertical centering, so it renders **top-aligned** relative to the taller button row. The Queue panel's title has `VerticalAlignment="Center"` and renders centered.

**Fix:** Add `VerticalAlignment="Center"` to the `panelTitle` `TextBlock` in ActivityColumn.

---

### 2. No gap between attachment path text and Reveal button

**File:** `src/VisualRelay.App/Views/Controls/TaskDetailPanel.axaml`, lines 274–280

```xml
<Button DockPanel.Dock="Right"
        Command="{Binding RevealCommand}"
        Padding="6,2"
        MinHeight="22"
        FontSize="11"
        Margin="0,0,6,0"
        Content="Reveal"/>
```

The `DockPanel` (line 267) has no `ColumnSpacing` equivalent. The `Reveal` button is `DockPanel.Dock="Right"` (line 274), and the `TextBlock` (line 281–287) fills the remaining space (`DockPanel` fill child). The `Reveal` button's `Margin="0,0,6,0"` has a gap only on its **right** (toward the `Remove` button). The **left** edge (toward the path text) has `0`, so the ellipsized path `TextBlock` butts directly against the `Reveal` button. The `TextTrimming="CharacterEllipsis"` prevents text overlap, but there's no visible gap.

**Fix:** Change `Margin="0,0,6,0"` to `Margin="6,0,6,0"` to restore ~6px gap on both sides.

---

### 3a. DRY grip-dot `:pointerover` styles (optional)

**File:** `src/VisualRelay.App/Views/MainWindow.axaml`, lines 145–153

```xml
<Style Selector="GridSplitter:pointerover /template/ Border#GripDot1">
  <Setter Property="Background" Value="#5B9CFF"/>
</Style>
<Style Selector="GridSplitter:pointerover /template/ Border#GripDot2">
  <Setter Property="Background" Value="#5B9CFF"/>
</Style>
<Style Selector="GridSplitter:pointerover /template/ Border#GripDot3">
  <Setter Property="Background" Value="#5B9CFF"/>
</Style>
```

Three identical selectors and setters. The test that validates the dot pattern (`ActivitySplitterAffordanceTests.cs`, lines 127–151: `Splitter_Grip_IsDotPattern_NotSolidBlock`) finds dots by **geometry** — `Bounds.Width >= 3 && Bounds.Width <= 6` — not by `Name`. The test explicitly checks `oldGrip` (named `"Grip"`) is null (line 133–135) but never references `GripDot1`, `GripDot2`, or `GripDot3`. So collapsing into a shared `Classes`-based selector (e.g., adding a `Classes="gripDot"` to all three Borders and using `Border.gripDot`) is **safe** — no existing test will break.

---

### 3b. Stale "Grid" references in test assertion messages

**File:** `tests/VisualRelay.Tests/TaskDetailRemoveButtonLayoutTests.cs`, lines 16–18 (class-level doc comment):

```csharp
/// *,Auto,Auto Grid inside a <see cref="Border"/> with Padding="4,3"
```

The template was changed from a `Grid` (with `ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto,Auto"`) to a `DockPanel` (line 267 of `TaskDetailPanel.axaml`). This class-level doc still references the old `Grid` structure. The assertion messages at lines 119–124 and 197–201 don't directly say "Grid", but the class summary is misleading for future failure diagnosis. The line number reference at line 124 (`"item template Border (line 265)"`) remains correct — line 265 is indeed `<Border Padding="4,3,10,3">`.

**Fix:** Update the class-level doc to say "DockPanel" instead of "Grid", and adjust the structural description to match the current DockPanel layout.

---

```json
{
  "evidence": "Four cosmetic issues confirmed across 4 files. (1) ActivityColumn.axaml:41-43 — panelTitle TextBlock missing VerticalAlignment=\"Center\" (QueuePanel:15-18 has it, causing visible misalignment with taller Reveal button in same Auto-height row). (2) TaskDetailPanel.axaml:279 — Reveal button Margin=\"0,0,6,0\" has zero left gap; file-path TextBlock (DockPanel fill child at line 281) butts directly against button. (3a) MainWindow.axaml:145-153 — three identical GripDot pointerover styles; test at ActivitySplitterAffordanceTests.cs:138-141 finds dots by geometry (Bounds.Width/Height), not by Name, so DRY via shared Classes selector is safe. (3b) TaskDetailRemoveButtonLayoutTests.cs:17 — class doc still says \"*,Auto,Auto Grid\" from before Grid→DockPanel refactor; assertion messages need updated wording.",
  "excerpts": [
    "ActivityColumn.axaml:41-43 <TextBlock Grid.Column=\"2\" Text=\"ACTIVITY\" Classes=\"panelTitle\"/> — no VerticalAlignment (cf QueuePanel:15-18 VerticalAlignment=\"Center\")",
    "TaskDetailPanel.axaml:274-280 Reveal button Margin=\"0,0,6,0\" — left margin is 0, no gap from path TextBlock (DockPanel fill child at line 281)",
    "MainWindow.axaml:145-153 three identical Style Selector=\"GridSplitter:pointerover /template/ Border#GripDot1/2/3\" — all set Background=\"#5B9CFF\"",
    "ActivitySplitterAffordanceTests.cs:138-141 finds dots by Bounds 3-6px range, not by Name — GripDot names are untested",
    "TaskDetailRemoveButtonLayoutTests.cs:17 \"*,Auto,Auto Grid inside a Border\" — stale after Grid→DockPanel change at TaskDetailPanel.axaml:267"
  ],
  "repro": "n/a — cosmetic issues visible in static code review; no runtime error to reproduce"
}
```
